chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male presented with shortness of breath and chest pain.
history: The patient received anticoagulation therapy for 3 months without improvement. He had a history of smoking and was a heavy smoker for 40 years.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed bilateral wheezing and decreased breath sounds.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: D-dimer elevated. PET-CT revealed hypermetabolic lesions suspicious for malignancy. Bronchoscopy with biopsy confirmed small cell lung cancer.
correct_diagnosis: Small cell lung cancer invading the pulmonary artery
differentials: Small cell lung cancer invading the pulmonary artery
correct_ranking: Small cell lung cancer invading the pulmonary artery
